injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Expected available for the tournament; minor flare-up reported with club but no extended absence projected

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Stretchered off in the first half with hamstring injury

injuryUpdates.source: Iraq Xtra (verified reports) and Transfermarkt - April/May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Forced off early in the playoff vs Bolivia. Initial estimate ~1 month, but a follow-up update described a milder recurrence at club level. Returned to club training in early May; likely fit for the tournament but worth monitoring.

squadChanges.details: Nashville SC midfielder, 22, born in Sweden, switched eligibility to Iraq under family-lineage rules. Approved by the global governing body on 12 May 2026 and added to the player pool for the tournament.
squadChanges.source: ESPN, Washington Post, Arab News - 12 May 2026

squadChanges.details: Dundee United defender, 20, born in Finland, eligibility switch approved on 12 May 2026 under family-lineage rules. Adds defensive depth to Arnold's squad.
squadChanges.source: ESPN, Washington Post, Arab News - 12 May 2026

bettingOddsUpdate.source: TheLines.com / DraftKings (Group I outright market) - May 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Iraq are heavy underdogs in Group I (France -235 favourite; Norway +280; Senegal +800; Iraq +4000 to win the group). Tournament-winner price quoted around 1500/1, equivalent to roughly +150000. Specific group-stage-exit market not located on consulted aggregators.

coachUpdate.source: Iraqi News Agency, Al Jazeera - April/May 2026
coachUpdate.notes: Arnold firmly in place; press conference scheduled at the IFA headquarters in Baghdad around 19-20 May to outline the final preparation programme before the Spain training camp departure on 22 May.

summary: Iraq's most material developments since 16 April are off-pitch: the global governing body approved two eligibility switches on 12 May, adding Nashville SC midfielder Ahmed Qasem and Dundee United defender Dario Naamo to Arnold's player pool. A short-lived US-visa scare for five players (including striker Ali Al-Hamadi) was publicly resolved by both the State Department and the IFA on 13 May, with the federation confirming all players hold valid visas. No new matches have been played; Iraq begin a Spain training camp on 18 May with confirmed warm-ups vs Andorra (29 May, Spain), Spain (4 June, Riazor) and Ivory Coast (8 June, Chicago).
